frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Aaj kal ke zamane mein sab fast food, convenience ke peeche bhaag rahe hain. Par Maa ke haath ka jo swaad hota hai na, uski baat hi kuch aur hai. Unhone apni poori zindagi ghar aur parivar ke liye laga di, bina kisi complaint ke, bas pyaar se khana banaya. Woh dedication, woh mamta ab kahan dikhti hai? Ghar ka asli sukh toh uss garma garam roti mein hi tha.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment glorifies the mother's domestic labor (cooking and self-sacrifice) as pure love and duty, explicitly stating she did it 'bina kisi complaint ke, bas pyaar se' which erases the labor aspect. It then implicitly compares current women (wives/DILs) unfavorably by asking 'Woh dedication, woh mamta ab kahan dikhti hai?', setting an impossible standard for the next generation based on the mother's 'tireless service to family'.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married. A Brahmin man in Pune. You are commenting under your real name on a news article shared on Facebook about a college in Maharashtra that introduced a dress code banning jeans and sleeveless tops for female students. Most commenters are criticising the college. You are writing a comment defending the dress code and explaining why you think institutions have the right and responsibility to regulate how young women dress and what happens in societies where there are no such standards.
